pr_tjc.similarWhy: Potomac's WM Synergy does Oracle/JD Edwards ERP consulting — similar mid-market operational tech. We help Potomac with Copilot AI readiness across their portfolio.
pr_tjc.insight: Highest score (92) in our pipeline. Capstone Logistics already runs Power BI and Microsoft stack — natural Copilot deployment. Sightline Intelligence (acquired Apr 2026) is literally an AI/ML analytics platform. The Microsoft footprint across their portfolio is substantial.
pr_tjc.emailSubject: Capstone's Power BI + Sightline's AI — connecting the dots
pr_tjc.emailBody:
Hi {{FIRST_NAME}},

TJC's portfolio caught my attention: Capstone Logistics already runs Power BI and Microsoft stack with Boomi integration, and you just brought in Sightline Intelligence — a cloud AI/ML analytics platform — in April.

There's a natural connection: Copilot for Microsoft 365 deployed at Capstone could unlock operational intelligence from the same data Sightline is already processing. Instead of two separate analytics stacks, you get one AI-augmented workflow.

We build exactly these kinds of cross-portfolio AI integrations for PE firms. Potomac Equity Partners is a good example — we helped them with Copilot readiness assessments across their tech services portfolio and found 30%+ efficiency gains in the first quarter.

Given that Capstone is already on Microsoft and Sightline is AI-native, the integration path is unusually clean. Worth 15 minutes to walk through how it works?

Best,
Jonathan Hicks
Incworx | AI & Microsoft Consulting for PE
pr_tjc.phone: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, this is Jonathan from Incworx — we do AI and Microsoft consulting specifically for PE firms. TJC scored highest in our PE research — Capstone Logistics is already on Power BI and Microsoft, and you just brought Sightline Intelligence in as an AI/ML analytics platform. There's a natural Copilot integration play between the two. We do this for firms like Potomac. Worth 15 minutes?

holdings: Elementis — SAP, custom ERP, specialty chemicals
BrightView Landscape — Salesforce, fleet mgmt, GPS
Eat Happy Group — POS, supply chain mgmt
pr_onerock.similarWhy: Pritzker has nearly identical portfolio profile — ProAmpac runs SAP in manufacturing, Buckman does chemicals with IoT. We help Pritzker integrate Microsoft AI with SAP-based operations.
pr_onerock.insight: Elementis runs SAP in specialty chemicals (almost identical to Pritzker's Buckman). BrightView is #1 commercial landscaping with Salesforce + fleet management — ripe for AI-powered route optimization. High score (90) because the portfolio maps directly to our Pritzker playbook.
pr_onerock.emailSubject: Elementis + SAP — same playbook as another PE firm we work with
pr_onerock.emailBody:
Hi {{FIRST_NAME}},

One Rock's Elementis caught my eye — specialty chemicals company running SAP for ERP. We work with a PE firm that owns Buckman, a nearly identical profile (specialty chemicals, SAP-based, industrial operations). The AI integration work we did there — Microsoft Copilot layered onto SAP data for supply chain forecasting and quality control — generated measurable efficiency gains in the first quarter.

BrightView is a different but equally interesting opportunity. #1 commercial landscaping with Salesforce and fleet management — the route optimization and predictive scheduling AI use case is high-ROI and deploys quickly.

Would it be useful to see the SAP + Microsoft AI framework we built for a comparable chemicals company? It's directly transferable to Elementis, and I can speak to the BrightView field services angle as well. 15 minutes — happy to share the actual results.

Best,
Jonathan Hicks
Incworx | AI & Microsoft Consulting for PE
pr_onerock.phone: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, Jonathan from Incworx. One Rock's Elementis is almost identical to a portfolio company we work with at another PE firm — specialty chemicals on SAP. The AI integration work we did there generated measurable results in the first quarter. BrightView is equally interesting from a field services AI angle. Worth 15 minutes to share the playbook?

The opportunity: Microsoft Fabric is emerging as a unified analytics platform that consolidates data integration, warehousing, and AI — potentially simplifying the multi-tool analytics stacks that companies like Klaviyo and GRESB manage today.

We work with Bow River Capital on cloud strategy for their SaaS portfolio companies — TrackVia, CloudShare — where data infrastructure decisions directly impact product roadmap and margins.

Would 15 minutes to walk through how Microsoft Fabric fits into a PE portfolio data strategy make sense? Especially relevant for GRESB's ESG analytics.

Best,
Jonathan Hicks
Incworx | AI & Microsoft Consulting for PE

pr_seaport.emailSubject: Cyberlink ASP — managed hosting to Azure managed services
pr_seaport.emailBody:
Hi {{FIRST_NAME}},

Seaport's comms infrastructure thesis is interesting — FMC GlobalSat in satellite networking, Cyberlink ASP in managed hosting, and On Target Media in lead gen analytics.

The evolution I'd flag: Cyberlink ASP's managed hosting business is positioned for an Azure migration play. The managed services market is shifting from traditional hosting to cloud-managed services, and companies that make that transition see both higher margins and better customer retention.

On Target Media's lead gen analytics could also benefit from AI/ML integration — predictive modeling for DRTV response rates and automated campaign optimization.

We work with PE firms whose portfolios include IT services companies (like Gryphon Investors with Fortreum and 3Cloud), and the managed services transformation playbook is well-established.

Would 15 minutes to walk through the Azure managed services transition and what it means for Cyberlink's growth trajectory?

Best,
Jonathan Hicks
Incworx | AI & Microsoft Consulting for PE
